summary_findings: Model rotation exists at launcher level, but downstream child enforcement relies heavily on prompt/instruction propagation.
Launcher bans cheap models for metaswarm commander launch and records premium/banned pools in fleet/state metadata.
Potential silent fallback risk when custom --agent-cmd omits {model}, causing underlying CLI defaults to take over.
Documentation has premium/default-model consistency gaps (README vs AGENTS).
Fallback-violation language is strongest for metaswarm downgrade scenarios; broader runtime fallback handling is less explicit.
consensus_notes: Consensus across successful workers: premium policy is clearly documented and injected, but not fully hard-enforced at every child boundary.
Consensus: metaswarm should report partial/failed rather than silently downgrade.
conflict_notes: No direct technical conflicts in successful worker findings; coverage gap exists because wkr-008-002 failed to deliver file-level audit.
blockers: wkr-008-002 returned refusal response and did not audit assigned files.
Task tool calls completed synchronously; no background agent IDs were returned by the tool interface in this run, so launch_started events used placeholder sync IDs.
